scenarioDescription: A healthcare startup wants to build a virtual assistant that answers patient questions by searching their internal medical policy documents. The system must retrieve relevant passages from approved documents and generate grounded, natural language responses without hallucinating.
toolboxServices: Amazon Bedrock|Amazon Kendra|Amazon Lex|Amazon Comprehend|Amazon S3|Amazon Textract|Amazon OpenSearch Service
hint: Think about where the approved documents must be stored before any indexing or retrieval can happen.
hint2: The last step uses a foundation model to generate answers from the retrieved passages.
explanation: Documents are stored in Amazon S3 as the durable source of truth. Amazon Kendra indexes those documents and performs intelligent passage retrieval (the R in RAG). Amazon Bedrock then uses a foundation model to generate a grounded natural language answer from the retrieved context, completing the Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline.

scenarioDescription: An airline receives millions of customer feedback forms annually. They need to automatically detect the language of each submission, determine whether the sentiment is positive or negative, and route all negative feedback to a priority support queue for immediate human follow-up.
toolboxServices: Amazon Comprehend|Amazon Translate|Amazon SQS|Amazon Rekognition|AWS Lambda|Amazon Polly|Amazon SNS
hint: One AWS AI service can detect language, sentiment, and key phrases all in a single API call.
hint2: A serverless compute service applies business logic to decide which feedback gets priority routing.
explanation: Amazon Comprehend detects the dominant language and performs sentiment analysis on each feedback form in one step. AWS Lambda evaluates the Comprehend output and applies routing logic — if sentiment is negative, it forwards the message. Amazon SQS receives the negative feedback as a priority queue for human agents to process immediately.

scenarioDescription: A university wants to make 20 years of recorded lecture videos searchable by topic. They need to convert speech to text, identify the academic subjects discussed in each lecture, and allow professors to find specific content using natural language search queries.
toolboxServices: Amazon Transcribe|Amazon Comprehend|Amazon Kendra|Amazon Rekognition|Amazon S3|Amazon Polly|Amazon Translate
hint: Before any text analysis can begin, the audio in the video files must be converted to written transcripts.
hint2: After topic extraction, a managed intelligent search service enables natural language querying across all transcripts.
explanation: Lecture videos are stored in Amazon S3. Amazon Transcribe converts the audio tracks to text transcripts using ASR. Amazon Comprehend performs topic modeling and key phrase extraction to tag each transcript with academic subjects. Amazon Kendra indexes the enriched transcripts and provides an intelligent natural language search experience for professors.

scenarioDescription: A law firm needs to digitize 50,000 scanned legal contracts, extract structured information such as party names, effective dates, and termination clauses, and store the results in a searchable database for compliance officers to query during audits.
toolboxServices: Amazon Textract|Amazon Comprehend|Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon Rekognition|Amazon S3|Amazon Translate|Amazon Athena
hint: Scanned paper documents must be stored in object storage before OCR processing can begin.
hint2: After extracting raw text from images, an NLP service identifies specific entity types like names and dates.
explanation: Scanned contracts are stored in Amazon S3. Amazon Textract performs OCR to extract raw text, tables, and form fields from the scanned images. Amazon Comprehend applies Named Entity Recognition to identify party names, dates, and organizations. The structured output is stored in Amazon DynamoDB for fast, queryable access during compliance audits.

scenarioDescription: A factory wants to detect defective widgets on their production line using overhead cameras. They need to collect images, have humans label them as defective or acceptable, train a custom image classification model, and run inference directly on the factory floor without cloud connectivity.
toolboxServices: Amazon Rekognition|Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth|Amazon SageMaker|AWS Panorama|Amazon S3|Amazon Bedrock|Amazon Lookout for Vision
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth|Amazon SageMaker|AWS Panorama
hint: Raw images from cameras must be collected in central storage before any labeling can begin.
hint2: After training, the model must be deployed to an edge device for inference without internet access.
explanation: Factory images are stored in Amazon S3. Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth manages the human labeling workflow to classify each image. Amazon SageMaker trains a custom image classification model on the labeled data. AWS Panorama deploys the model to edge appliances on the factory floor for real-time, offline inference without requiring cloud connectivity.

scenarioDescription: A financial institution wants to fine-tune a foundation model on their proprietary earnings report corpus to improve summarization quality. Regulatory requirements mandate that all training data must remain within the customer's AWS account and never be shared with the model provider.
toolboxServices: Amazon Bedrock|Amazon SageMaker JumpStart|Amazon S3|Amazon Comprehend|AWS Lambda|Amazon Kendra|Amazon SageMaker
hint: Proprietary training data must be stored securely in your own AWS account before customization begins.
hint2: The fully managed foundation model service keeps your fine-tuning data private and never shares it with model providers.
explanation: Proprietary earnings reports are stored in Amazon S3 within the customer's account. Amazon Bedrock performs custom model fine-tuning using that data while guaranteeing the training data is never shared with third-party model providers and remains within the customer's account, satisfying regulatory requirements. Bedrock handles the full lifecycle as a managed service.

scenarioDescription: A global media company needs to transcribe live TV broadcast audio in real-time, translate the transcript into 12 languages simultaneously, and push each translated version to regional subscriber feeds around the world.
toolboxServices: Amazon Transcribe|Amazon Translate|Amazon Polly|Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|Amazon Comprehend|AWS Lambda|Amazon SNS
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|Amazon Transcribe|Amazon Translate|Amazon SNS
hint: Live streaming audio must first be ingested by a real-time data streaming service before any processing.
hint2: After translation, a pub/sub messaging service fans out the results to multiple regional subscribers.
explanation: Live audio is ingested via Amazon Kinesis Data Streams for real-time buffering. Amazon Transcribe performs streaming speech-to-text conversion. Amazon Translate converts the transcript into 12 target languages. Amazon SNS fans out each translated version to regional subscriber endpoints for parallel global delivery.

scenarioDescription: A grocery chain wants to predict weekly product demand for 500 stores over the next 60 days. Their team has zero ML experience and needs a fully managed, no-code forecasting solution that automatically selects the best algorithm from their historical sales data.
toolboxServices: Amazon Forecast|Amazon SageMaker|Amazon S3|Amazon QuickSight|Amazon Bedrock|AWS Glue|Amazon SageMaker Autopilot
hint: Historical sales CSV data must be uploaded to storage before the forecasting service can access it.
hint2: After forecasts are generated, a BI visualization tool lets store managers explore predicted demand interactively.
explanation: Historical sales data is uploaded to Amazon S3. Amazon Forecast is the correct no-code choice because it automatically evaluates multiple time-series algorithms (DeepAR+, ETS, ARIMA) and selects the best one — requiring zero ML expertise. Amazon QuickSight visualizes the forecast results in interactive dashboards for store managers to plan inventory.

scenarioDescription: A social media company must automatically moderate user-uploaded photos for policy-violating content. When the AI model's confidence is below 90%, the image must be routed to a human reviewer. All moderation decisions — both automated and human — must be logged for legal audit trails.
toolboxServices: Amazon Rekognition|Amazon Comprehend|AWS Lambda|Amazon A2I (Augmented AI)|Amazon S3|Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon Bedrock
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|Amazon Rekognition|Amazon A2I (Augmented AI)|Amazon DynamoDB
hint: Uploaded images must be stored in durable object storage before any content analysis begins.
hint2: A human-in-the-loop service automatically routes low-confidence predictions to human reviewers.
explanation: User photos are stored in Amazon S3. Amazon Rekognition Content Moderation detects policy-violating content and returns confidence scores. Amazon A2I routes images with confidence below 90% to human reviewers for final judgment. All decisions are logged in Amazon DynamoDB to maintain a complete, queryable audit trail for legal compliance.

scenarioDescription: An enterprise wants a generative AI assistant where employees ask natural language questions about internal data spread across Confluence, SharePoint, Salesforce, and PostgreSQL databases. The system must enforce existing document-level access controls so each employee only sees data they are authorized to view.
toolboxServices: Amazon Bedrock|Amazon Kendra|Amazon Q Business|Amazon Comprehend|Amazon OpenSearch Service|AWS IAM Identity Center|Amazon Lex
correctPipelineOrder: AWS IAM Identity Center|Amazon Q Business
hint: User identity and permissions must be established before any data retrieval occurs.
hint2: AWS offers a fully managed generative AI assistant that natively connects to 40+ enterprise data sources and respects document-level ACLs.
explanation: AWS IAM Identity Center authenticates each employee and establishes their group memberships and permissions. Amazon Q Business is the correct choice because it natively connects to 40+ data sources (Confluence, SharePoint, Salesforce, databases), automatically enforces document-level ACLs, and generates answers using RAG — ensuring employees only see information they are authorized to access.

scenarioDescription: A pharmaceutical company needs to extract drug names, dosages, medical conditions, and adverse reactions from 100,000 clinical trial reports. The extracted medical entities must be stored in a graph database so researchers can discover relationships between drugs and side effects.
toolboxServices: Amazon Comprehend Medical|Amazon Comprehend|Amazon Neptune|Amazon Textract|Amazon S3|Amazon Bedrock|Amazon Kendra
hint: These are text-based clinical reports, not scanned images — start with storage, not OCR.
hint2: Medical entity extraction requires the healthcare-specialized NLP service, not the general-purpose one.
explanation: Clinical trial reports are stored in Amazon S3. Amazon Comprehend Medical (not standard Comprehend) is purpose-built to extract medical entities like drug names (RxNorm), conditions (ICD-10), dosages, and adverse reactions from medical text. Amazon Neptune stores the entities and relationships as a knowledge graph, enabling researchers to traverse connections between drugs and side effects.

scenarioDescription: An insurance company wants to detect fraudulent claims by combining structured claims data with unstructured adjuster notes. They need to convert the notes into structured features and then use a managed fraud detection service that automatically trains models — no custom ML code required.
toolboxServices: Amazon Fraud Detector|Amazon SageMaker|Amazon Comprehend|Amazon S3|AWS Lambda|Amazon Macie|Amazon Lookout for Metrics
hint: Unstructured adjuster notes must be processed by an NLP service before they can be used as structured input features.
hint2: AWS provides a purpose-built managed service specifically designed for fraud detection that requires no custom ML code.
explanation: Claims data and adjuster notes are stored in Amazon S3. Amazon Comprehend extracts sentiment, key phrases, and entities from the unstructured notes, converting them into structured features. Amazon Fraud Detector is a fully managed service that automatically trains fraud detection models on the combined structured features and returns risk scores — no custom ML expertise required.

scenarioDescription: A delivery company streams GPS telemetry from 15,000 trucks in real-time. They need to ingest this high-volume data, enrich each record with current weather conditions, and send the enriched data to a deployed ML model that predicts estimated delivery times.
toolboxServices: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose|AWS Lambda|Amazon SageMaker|Amazon S3|Amazon Location Service|Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|AWS Lambda|Amazon SageMaker
hint: High-volume, real-time telemetry from thousands of sources requires a managed streaming ingestion service.
hint2: Between ingestion and ML inference, a serverless compute layer enriches each record with external weather data.
explanation: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams ingests the high-throughput GPS telemetry from 15,000 trucks with low latency. AWS Lambda consumes stream records and enriches each one with weather data from an external API, building the complete feature vector. The enriched data is sent to an Amazon SageMaker real-time endpoint that returns predicted delivery times.

scenarioDescription: A bank wants to automate mortgage applications. Customers upload scanned pay stubs, tax returns, and government IDs. The system must extract text from scans, compare the ID photo against a live selfie for identity verification, and store all verified data for the underwriting team.
toolboxServices: Amazon Textract|Amazon Rekognition|Amazon S3|Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon Comprehend|Amazon Lex|AWS Lambda
hint: Scanned financial documents require OCR to extract structured text like names, amounts, and dates.
hint2: Matching an ID card photo to a selfie requires a computer vision service with face comparison capabilities.
explanation: Uploaded documents and selfies are stored in Amazon S3. Amazon Textract performs OCR to extract structured text, tables, and form data from pay stubs, tax returns, and IDs. Amazon Rekognition CompareFaces verifies the applicant's identity by matching the ID photo against the live selfie. Verified data is stored in Amazon DynamoDB for the underwriting team.

scenarioDescription: A video streaming platform wants to generate personalized movie recommendations for 50 million users based on their viewing history. They need a fully managed recommendation service that automatically selects the best algorithm and requires no ML infrastructure management.
toolboxServices: Amazon Personalize|Amazon SageMaker|Amazon S3|Amazon Bedrock|Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose|Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon EMR
hint: User-item interaction data (viewing history) must be stored before the recommendation service can train.
hint2: AWS offers a fully managed recommendation engine that auto-selects the best algorithm — no ML expertise needed.
explanation: Viewing history is formatted as user-item interactions and stored in Amazon S3. Amazon Personalize automatically selects the optimal recommendation algorithm, trains the model, and creates a real-time campaign — all without ML expertise. Recommendations are cached in Amazon DynamoDB for sub-millisecond retrieval when users open the app.

scenarioDescription: A hospital system wants to predict which patients are at high risk of 30-day readmission using structured electronic health records. The data science team has no ML experience and needs an automated solution that builds, trains, compares, and deploys the best model without writing any code.
toolboxServices: Amazon SageMaker Autopilot|Amazon SageMaker|Amazon HealthLake|Amazon S3|Amazon Bedrock|Amazon Comprehend Medical|AWS Glue
hint: Raw EHR data typically requires cleaning and transformation before it is suitable for ML training.
hint2: A specific SageMaker capability automatically trains multiple models and selects the best one — zero coding required.
explanation: Structured EHR data is stored in Amazon S3. AWS Glue performs ETL to clean, normalize, and transform the data into ML-ready format. Amazon SageMaker Autopilot automatically explores the data, engineers features, trains multiple candidate models, ranks them by performance, and deploys the winner — all without writing a single line of ML code.

scenarioDescription: An autonomous vehicle company must train a deep learning model on petabytes of driving video. Training requires distributed GPU computing with high-throughput data access, and costs must be minimized using interruptible compute with automatic recovery.
toolboxServices: Amazon SageMaker|Amazon EC2 P4d Instances|Amazon S3|Amazon FSx for Lustre|AWS Batch|Amazon Bedrock|Amazon ECS
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|Amazon FSx for Lustre|Amazon SageMaker
hint: Petabytes of video need durable storage, but GPU training jobs require a faster file system layer on top.
hint2: A high-performance parallel file system can lazily load data from S3 to provide the throughput GPUs demand.
explanation: Petabytes of video are stored durably in Amazon S3. Amazon FSx for Lustre provides a high-performance parallel file system that lazily loads data from S3, delivering hundreds of GB/s throughput for distributed GPU training. Amazon SageMaker orchestrates multi-GPU distributed training with managed spot instances and automatic checkpointing, reducing costs by up to 90%.

scenarioDescription: A contact center wants to analyze 2 million recorded customer calls to identify trending complaints, measure per-agent sentiment scores, and detect calls where customers became frustrated. They need a single service that handles transcription, sentiment, and issue detection together.
toolboxServices: Amazon Transcribe Call Analytics|Amazon Connect|Amazon Comprehend|Amazon QuickSight|Amazon S3|Amazon Lex|Amazon Polly
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|Amazon Transcribe Call Analytics|Amazon QuickSight
hint: Call recordings must be stored before any speech analytics processing can begin.
hint2: AWS offers a specialized transcription service built specifically for call center audio with built-in sentiment and issue detection.
explanation: Call recordings are stored in Amazon S3. Amazon Transcribe Call Analytics is purpose-built for contact centers — it performs transcription, speaker diarization, per-turn sentiment analysis, issue detection, and call summarization in a single API. Amazon QuickSight visualizes the aggregated results so managers can track complaint trends and agent performance.

scenarioDescription: A security operations center needs to continuously monitor AWS account activity for anomalous behavior indicating potential breaches. When a high-severity threat is detected, the system must automatically isolate the compromised resource and notify the security team through a multi-step workflow.
toolboxServices: Amazon GuardDuty|Amazon SageMaker|Amazon S3|AWS Lambda|AWS Step Functions|Amazon Macie|Amazon Detective
hint: AWS provides a managed threat detection service that uses ML to continuously analyze CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs.
hint2: After threat detection, a serverless function evaluates severity before triggering a multi-step automated remediation workflow.
explanation: Amazon GuardDuty continuously monitors account activity using ML-based anomaly detection across CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs. When a finding is generated, AWS Lambda evaluates the severity and enriches the alert with context. AWS Step Functions orchestrates the automated remediation — isolating compromised instances, revoking credentials, and notifying the security team in a consistent, auditable workflow.

scenarioDescription: A multinational corporation wants to build a generative AI coding assistant that helps developers write, debug, and optimize code directly within their IDE. The assistant must understand the company's internal codebase, private APIs, and coding standards stored in their repositories.
toolboxServices: Amazon Q Developer|Amazon CodeWhisperer|Amazon Bedrock|Amazon CodeGuru|AWS Lambda|Amazon Kendra|Amazon SageMaker
hint: AWS offers a purpose-built AI coding assistant that can be customized with your organization's private codebase.
hint2: This service integrates directly into popular IDEs and can be trained on internal repositories and coding standards.
explanation: Amazon Q Developer is the correct single-service answer because it is a purpose-built generative AI coding assistant that integrates directly into IDEs, understands natural language instructions, and can be customized with the company's internal codebase, private APIs, and coding standards. It handles code generation, debugging, optimization, and explanation as a fully managed service — no custom pipeline assembly required.
